title: Six keys to the garden
summary: A small community garden in Berlin has six gardeners and six keys. One key is missing. Nobody wants to be the one to ask for it back.
body:
There is a small community garden behind an old building in Berlin. Six people share it. Each gardener has a key. The garden is not big. There are tomatoes, beans, three apple trees, and one bench.

This spring, one key was missing. The old woman, Frau Lange, asked at the first meeting, 'Who has the key of Anya?' Nobody answered.

Anya moved to Hamburg in the autumn for a new job. She left in a hurry. She did not return her key.

Frau Lange said, 'I will write to her.' She wrote a short letter. Anya did not reply.

A young gardener named Emil said, 'I can change the lock. It is not expensive.' But Frau Lange said, 'Then five of us will have new keys, and Anya will have an old one. The garden will not be the same garden.'

The other gardeners were quiet. They understood. A key is a small thing. A welcome is not.

In June, Anya came to Berlin for one weekend. She walked to the garden. The key was still in her bag. She opened the gate. The tomatoes were tall. The bench was empty. She sat down. She stayed for one hour.

When she left, she put the key on the bench. She wrote a note next to it: 'I am sorry I was slow. Thank you for keeping the door.' Frau Lange found the note the next morning. She did not change the lock that year.

annotations.meaningVi: vội vàng — cụm cố định: be/leave in a hurry = (đi/rời) trong vội vã
trả lại chìa khóa của cô ấy

annotations.phrase: I am sorry I was slow
annotations.meaningVi: Tôi xin lỗi vì đã chậm trễ
giữ cánh cửa (cho ai) — ý nghĩa: giữ chỗ, không khóa người khác bên ngoài
cây táo (danh từ kết hợp: apple + trees)
chuyển đến Hamburg (quá khứ đơn: move + to + địa danh)
một chìa khóa bị thiếu (cấu trúc bị động ngầm: be + missing = bị mất, bị thiếu)

grammarNotes.title: 'will' for decisions made at the moment of speaking
grammarNotes.bodyVi: 'Will' dùng cho quyết định nảy ra ngay khi nói (không có kế hoạch trước). Trong bài: 'I will write to her' — Frau Lange quyết định ngay tại cuộc họp. 'I can change the lock' dùng 'can' (khả năng / đề nghị). Khác với 'be going to' chỉ kế hoạch đã có sẵn. Cấu trúc: chủ ngữ + will + động từ nguyên thể.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: 'Will' is for decisions made at the moment of speaking, not for pre-planned actions. From the passage: 'I will write to her' — Frau Lange decides this at the meeting itself. 'I can change the lock' uses 'can' for ability or offer. Compare with 'be going to' (pre-planned). Structure: subject + will + base verb.

translationVi:
Có một khu vườn cộng đồng nhỏ sau một tòa nhà cũ ở Berlin. Sáu người cùng dùng chung. Mỗi người làm vườn có một chìa khóa. Khu vườn không lớn. Có cà chua, đậu, ba cây táo và một chiếc ghế dài.

Mùa xuân năm nay, một chìa khóa bị thiếu. Bà cụ Frau Lange hỏi ở cuộc họp đầu tiên: 'Ai giữ chìa khóa của Anya?' Không ai trả lời.

Anya chuyển đến Hamburg vào mùa thu vì có công việc mới. Cô ấy đi vội. Cô ấy không trả lại chìa khóa.

Frau Lange nói: 'Tôi sẽ viết thư cho cô ấy.' Bà viết một lá thư ngắn. Anya không trả lời.

Một người làm vườn trẻ tên Emil nói: 'Tôi có thể thay ổ khóa. Nó không đắt đâu.' Nhưng Frau Lange nói: 'Vậy thì năm người chúng ta sẽ có chìa mới, còn Anya sẽ giữ chìa cũ. Khu vườn sẽ không còn là khu vườn cũ nữa.'

Những người khác im lặng. Họ hiểu. Một chiếc chìa khóa là chuyện nhỏ. Một lời chào đón thì không.

Thang Sáu, Anya về Berlin một cuối tuần. Cô đi bộ đến khu vườn. Chìa khóa vẫn còn trong túi xách. Cô mở cổng. Cà chua đã cao. Ghế dài trống. Cô ngồi xuống. Cô ở lại một giờ.

Khi rời đi, cô đặt chìa khóa lên ghế. Cô viết một mảnh giấy bên cạnh: 'Tôi xin lỗi vì đã chậm. Cảm ơn vì đã giữ cánh cửa.' Frau Lange tìm thấy mảnh giấy vào sáng hôm sau. Năm đó bà không thay ổ khóa.

questions.prompt: Where is the garden, and how many people share it?
questions.explanation: 'There is a small community garden behind an old building in Berlin. Six people share it.'

questions.prompt: Why did Anya not return her key?
options: She moved to Hamburg in a hurry for a new job
She gave it to a friend
questions.explanation: 'Anya moved to Hamburg in the autumn for a new job. She left in a hurry.'

questions.prompt: What did Frau Lange do first?
questions.explanation: 'I will write to her. She wrote a short letter.'

options: Change the lock; it is not expensive
questions.explanation: 'I can change the lock. It is not expensive.'

questions.prompt: Why did Frau Lange refuse Emil's idea?
options: Because Anya would have an old key while the others had new ones — 'the garden will not be the same garden.'

questions.prompt: What does the passage mean by 'A key is a small thing. A welcome is not.'?
options: Belonging to a place matters more than the physical key.
questions.explanation: Contrast: small thing (key) vs. large thing (welcome).
'In June, Anya came to Berlin for one weekend.'

questions.prompt: What did Anya do in the garden?
options: She sat on the bench for one hour
questions.explanation: 'She sat down. She stayed for one hour.'

options: The key and a note on the bench
questions.explanation: 'She put the key on the bench. She wrote a note next to it.'

questions.prompt: What did Frau Lange do that year?
options: She did not change the lock
questions.explanation: 'She did not change the lock that year.'

options: Waiting for someone to come back can be a quiet act of community.
Locks should always be changed when a key is lost.
Community gardens should be very large.
Berlin has the best apple trees.
questions.explanation: Frau Lange's refusal to change the lock is the heart of the story.

The other gardeners were quiet. They understood. A key is a small thing. A {{b6}} is not.

In June, Anya came to Berlin for one weekend. She walked to the garden. The key was still in her {{b7}}. She opened the gate. The tomatoes were tall. The bench was empty. She sat down. She stayed for one hour.

When she left, she put the key on the {{b8}}. She wrote a note next to it: 'I am sorry I was slow. Thank you for keeping the {{b9}}.' Frau Lange found the note the next morning. She did not change the lock that {{b10}}.

blanks.explanation: 'I will write to her' — decision at the moment.
A welcome is not a small thing.
The key was still in her bag.
She put the key on the bench.
Thank you for keeping the door.
Did not change the lock that year.

criticalThinkingQuestion: Frau Lange says, 'Then five of us will have new keys, and Anya will have an old one. The garden will not be the same garden.' Have you ever been on either side of an unreturned key — a half-kept promise to come back, or someone else's promise to you? What did the people on the other side decide to do with it?
